definition: Credit is assessed as the most mispriced asset class with equity-implied vs actual HY spread divergence of 50-75bp. The directional claim that credit widens has support from Minsky fragility data and covenant-lite dynamics, but timing is highly uncertain (covenant-lite could extend status quo 6-12 months beyond expectation). Direction confidence: 6/10, timing confidence: 4/10.
evidence: Equity-implied vs actual HY spread divergence of 50-75bp
Minsky fragility data supports widening direction
Covenant-lite structures could delay repricing 6-12 months
75% base rate from n=4 refuted as statistically meaningless (Wilson CI 22-99%)
Pair_2 noted inconsistency: credit reprices while equity escapes contradicts recession scenario
open_questions: What is the specific catalyst that forces credit repricing?
Can private credit absorption indefinitely delay public market repricing?
